three_laws.title: How you say it matters more than what you say.
three_laws.body: Delivery, energy, confidence, pacing, filler words. A mediocre answer delivered with conviction beats a perfect answer delivered with uncertainty.

three_laws.title: Flip your weakness into a strength.
three_laws.body: Every answer is an opportunity to address a perceived weakness proactively before it becomes a rejection reason.

three_laws.title: Don't volunteer information that can hurt you.
three_laws.body: Never badmouth employers. Never over-explain gaps. Never share compensation. Never volunteer failures you weren't asked about.

five_shifts.title: They test whether you've BUILT AI, not whether you UNDERSTAND it.
five_shifts.body: If the candidate can't name architectures, eval metrics, and business impact, flag it.

five_shifts.title: Vibe coding is a real round now.
five_shifts.body: Google, Figma, Perplexity, and v0 all require it. Cursor, Lovable, Bolt, Claude Code, Replit Agent.

five_shifts.title: AI product sense replaced traditional product sense.
five_shifts.body: Candidates must separate model-layer from app-layer, address safety proactively, and prioritize with math.

five_shifts.title: AI system design is the newest round.
five_shifts.body: 18 months old. Catches the most candidates off guard. Zero public resources except the DASME framework.

five_shifts.title: Safety is tested everywhere.
five_shifts.body: Anthropic has a dedicated round. OpenAI embeds it. If a candidate doesn't mention safety in a case answer — flag it.

seven_rounds.label: Behavioral
seven_rounds.tested: Who you are, how you work

eight_dimensions.name: Timing & Conciseness
eight_dimensions.detail: 90s–2min behavioral, 3–5min case sections. Going over 2 min is the #1 rejection reason. Estimate length at ~150 words/min.

eight_dimensions.name: Structure & Clarity
eight_dimensions.detail: Did they answer the question asked? Clear beginning/middle/end? For cases: framework before diving in?

eight_dimensions.name: Specific Impact
eight_dimensions.detail: Concrete metrics and outcomes? For AI PM: MODEL metrics (precision, recall, F1, latency, cost/inference) alongside business metrics.

eight_dimensions.name: Confidence vs. Hedging
eight_dimensions.detail: Flag every 'I think,' 'kind of,' 'maybe,' 'sort of,' 'probably,' 'a little bit,' 'not sure if,' 'I feel like,' uptalk.

eight_dimensions.name: Filler Words
eight_dimensions.detail: Count every um, uh, like, so, actually, basically, you know, I mean, right, yeah.

eight_dimensions.name: Weakness Flipping
eight_dimensions.detail: Did the answer proactively address likely concerns? 10 = every weakness addressed elegantly, 1 = ignored or made worse.

eight_dimensions.name: Information Control
eight_dimensions.detail: Badmouthing? Over-explaining gaps? Sharing comp? Volunteering damaging info?

eight_dimensions.name: Memorability
eight_dimensions.detail: Cocktail-party test: could the interviewer retell this story to a friend?
